title: LATAM Remote Patient Monitoring Gap
problem: Remote patient monitoring (RPM) is almost non-existent in LATAM. Chronic disease patients only monitored during office visits. No continuous vital sign tracking. Complications develop between visits. No alerting for deterioration. COVID-19 showed need for home monitoring but infrastructure doesn't exist.
solution: RPM platform for LATAM. Integration with low-cost connected devices (BP monitors, glucometers, pulse oximeters). Mobile app for data transmission. Clinician dashboard with alerts. AI-powered trend analysis. Integration with EMR. Focus on chronic conditions (diabetes, hypertension, heart failure).
technology: IoT device integration, mobile app, clinician dashboard, alert engine, ML trend analysis, EMR integration, FHIR APIs
target_customer: Health insurers (reduce hospitalizations), primary care practices, chronic care management programs, hospital discharge programs
ai_type: Trend analysis, anomaly detection, risk scoring
traction_signals: Chronic disease burden in LATAM massive
regulatory_notes: Medical device regulations, telehealth monitoring regulations, data privacy
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - RPM is infrastructure for chronic care. Technical but defensible. Device-agnostic platform strategy. Recurring revenue from insurers and practices.
notes: RPM is technical but critical for chronic care. Device costs are falling. Platform approach (integrate many devices) is key. Insurers will pay to reduce hospitalizations.
